title: 2025 Annual Report / Form 10-K
note: Primary source for Wabtec's business segments, financial condition, installed-base context, goodwill, acquired technology, contracts, and profitability evidence.

title: Locomotive
note: Primary product source for Wabtec's locomotive portfolio, components, braking systems, digital intelligence offerings, and service capabilities.

title: FLXdrive Battery-Electric Locomotive
note: Documents Wabtec's battery-electric heavy-haul locomotive capabilities, modular battery architecture, regenerative charging, and charging options.

title: Trip Optimizer
note: Documents Wabtec's train energy-management software, fuel-saving claims, PTC/ATP integration, air-brake advisement, and automation features.

title: ECP-4200 Electronically Controlled Pneumatic Freight Car Brake System
note: Primary product source for Wabtec's ECP braking system and its claimed stopping-distance, fuel-efficiency, and capacity benefits.

title: Wabtec Market Capitalization
note: Market data source for Wabtec's reported May 2026 market capitalization and market-cap history.

title: Wabtec PE Ratio 2012-2026
note: Market-data source for a recent Wabtec P/E ratio estimate used as a volatile valuation input.

title: OpenRail Association
note: Source for the open-source railway software ecosystem and neutral collaboration model relevant to rail software alternatives.

title: OpenRail Association Annual Report 2025
note: Provides context on OpenRail's active projects, governance, and production-oriented open-source railway software work.

title: OpenRailwayMap
note: Source for an open railway infrastructure map based on OpenStreetMap and OpenHistoricalMap data.

title: OpenRailwayMap GitHub Repository
note: Repository source for OpenRailwayMap code and its open-source implementation details.

title: Open Source Ecology
note: Evidence source for open hardware and distributed production concepts that could enable local fabrication and refurbishment workflows.

title: Open-source hardware
note: Background source on open hardware concepts used to frame distributed manufacturing and repairability mechanisms.
